preamble: Deaspiration is the loss of the puff of air that follows an aspirated stop. This typically happens when the stop is not in a stressed, syllable-initial position (e.g., after /s/ in English), or when loanwords are adapted into languages that lack aspiration, like French or Spanish.

examples: Adaptation of loanwords from languages with voiced aspiration
Adaptation of loanwords from languages with voiced aspiration

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
